nameEn: Actual distance to the danger point
actionEn: The plate states the exact distance to the danger point indicated by the main sign.

nameEn: Distance of the sign from start/end of road or lane
actionEn: The plate indicates the distance to the start or end of the relevant road/lane.
The plate states the length of the tunnel ahead.

nameEn: Length of the section with repeated danger
actionEn: The plate states the length of the section over which the danger indicated by the main sign continues.
The plate indicates that the warning sign's effect ends here.

nameEn: Direction and distance to the danger point
actionEn: The plate indicates the side and distance of the danger point.
The plate shows the actual layout of the intersection ahead — plan your route in advance.

nameEn: Actual direction of the priority road through the intersection
actionEn: The plate shows how the priority road bends inside the intersection.
The plate states how many dangerous bends are ahead.
The plate states the distance to the road narrowing.
The plate shows the procedure for entering the priority road.
The plate shows the actual layout of the roundabout ahead — choose your direction in advance.
The plate indicates the direction to the built-up area the main sign refers to.
The plate indicates how often the situation shown by the main sign occurs (e.g., number of children).

nameEn: Zone of effect of the prohibition sign
actionEn: The plate shows the zone (area) the prohibition sign above covers.
The plate depicts the specific danger point related to the main warning sign.
The plate gives information on the traffic light's operating mode (e.g., activates only when a pedestrian presses the button).
The plate indicates that the parking place is reserved for holders of priority-vehicle permits.
The plate states over what distance the sign above applies.
The plate additionally warns of an unexpected change in the road's direction.

nameEn: Vehicle type the sign applies to
actionEn: The plate indicates the sign applies only to the stated vehicle type.

nameEn: Sign's effect schedule (e.g., on working days)
actionEn: The plate indicates the sign above applies only on the stated days (e.g., working days).

nameEn: Distance from the sign to where the prohibition starts
actionEn: The plate states the distance to where the prohibition actually starts.

nameEn: Sign does not apply to single-track bicycles
actionEn: The plate indicates the prohibition above does not apply to single-track cyclists.
The plate states exactly which hours or days the sign above applies.

nameEn: Vehicle type the sign applies to (e.g., with a trailer)
actionEn: The plate indicates the sign applies only to vehicles with a trailer.
The plate clarifies which vehicle type the sign applies to.
The plate states the number of available parking spaces.
The plate indicates the direction to the nearest parking place.
The plate above the sign indicates that a toll is charged on this road section.
The plate indicates the parking place is reserved for holders of a disability permit.

nameEn: Method of positioning the vehicle at the roadside
actionEn: The plate shows at what angle or manner to park the vehicle at the roadside.
The plate indicates which parking method (e.g., partly on the pavement) is allowed.

nameEn: Zone of effect of the information sign
actionEn: The plate states to what extent (area or distance) the information sign above applies.

nameEn: Vehicle type exempted from the prohibition
actionEn: The plate indicates the stated vehicle type is exempt from the prohibition above (e.g., residents' vehicles).
The plate gives additional clarification for the prohibition or mandatory sign above.
